stories.title: GlobePanel — procedural earth texture + atmosphere glow + 2400 stars + 18 satellites + animated orbits
acceptanceCriteria: GlobePanel renders root div with className 'globe-wrap' (not 'panel-body globe') matching prototype CSS
Inner div with className 'globe-canvas' is the R3F mount point for the Three.js scene
Procedural earth texture function makeEarthTexture(w,h) creates ocean + ice caps + 18 continent blobs + city lights + cloud wisps
Bump texture function makeEarthBumpTexture(w,h) adds 8 mountain ranges as bright streaks
Star field layer renders ≥2000 background stars in a sphere shell
Atmosphere glow shader (BackSide, AdditiveBlending) wraps the earth at radius 1.06
Constants module exports SATELLITES (≥18 entries), THREAT_SITES (≥7 entries), FRIENDLY_EW (≥2 entries)
Satellites animate along orbital paths (phase advancement per RAF tick, 30x time scale)
Vitest test asserts SATELLITES.length === 18, THREAT_SITES.length === 7, FRIENDLY_EW.length === 2
Vitest test asserts GlobePanel renders divs with classNames 'globe-wrap', 'globe-canvas', and the four 'globe-hud-*' overlays

stories.title: GlobePanel — 4-corner HUD overlays + layer toggles + drag/wheel/auto-spin interactions
acceptanceCriteria: globe-hud-tl renders SPACE · ORBITAL VIEW + sats/threats/friendly/zoom counters (4 hud-rows)
globe-hud-tr renders 9-row layer toggle list with checkboxes
globe-hud-bl renders 위성 · LIVE table with first 8 satellites
globe-hud-br renders THREAT REGISTRY with all 7 threat sites
globe-credits text 'DRAG · 회전 · WHEEL · 줌' renders at bottom center
Layer toggle clicks update useState booleans and toggle THREE.Group .visible
Mouse drag rotates earth, wheel zooms camera, autoSpin advances rotation
Vitest test asserts all four globe-hud-* divs render with the right number of children

stories.title: GpsLosPanel — DOP grid (5 cells) + SNR bars (12 sats) + PDOP forecast SVG + 21-sat skyplot
acceptanceCriteria: GpsLosPanel renders root with className 'gps-panel' containing gps-skyplot (svg) + gps-side
Skyplot SVG renders ≥21 satellites across 4 constellations (GPS/GLO/GAL/BDS)
DOP grid renders 5 cells: PDOP/HDOP/VDOP/TDOP/GDOP
SNR bars render first 12 sats each with snr-id/snr-track/snr-fill/snr-val
PDOP forecast SVG renders polyline path + gradient fill + 2.0 limit dashed line
gps-counts strip shows USED/VIS/MASKED triplet
Vitest test asserts ≥21 satellite circles in skyplot SVG and 5 DOP cells render

stories.title: SpaceSheet container — full-bleed 60/40 split with proper height passing to GlobePanel
acceptanceCriteria: SpaceSheet root uses 'sheet space-sheet' className with display:flex, flexDirection:column, flex:1, minHeight:0
space-sheet-grid container has explicit height (flex:1, height:100%) so children fill vertically
Globe container .space-sheet-globe gets flex:'1 1 60%' AND height:100%
GpsLos container .space-sheet-gpslos gets flex:'0 0 40%', height:100%
Bottom telemetry strip uses 'space-sheet-strip' className
Vitest test renders <SpaceSheet /> and asserts space-sheet-grid + space-sheet-globe + space-sheet-gpslos divs all present
Vitest test asserts space-telemetry-strip exists with sat-count and threat-count showing 18 and 7

stories.title: GlobePanel canvas-fallback when WebGL unavailable
acceptanceCriteria: GlobePanel always renders an SVG fallback inside .globe-canvas (so jsdom is never blank)
SVG fallback uses className 'globe-canvas-fallback' with role='img' and aria-label
Earth circle uses radial gradient (deep blue → black) so it's visibly an earth in the SVG path
Vitest test in jsdom asserts globe-canvas-fallback renders an svg with a circle and orbit ellipses
When WebGL is available (production browser), useEffect creates Three.js scene as in prototype
